scope: Final pre-PR code review of Stage 4 (Supabase Auth + /admin dashboard + is_featured/is_hidden toggles + manual refresh + Stage 3 polish bundle). 19 new files + 8 modified files. QA P1s (3 items) confirmed fixed in implementation.
verdict_summary: No critical findings. Service-role key isolation verified (only imported from server-only modules). Every server action gates on assertAdmin() / allowlist. safeHref rejects javascript:/data:/file:/vbscript:/relative/empty. React auto-escapes the confirmation email echo (no dangerouslySetInnerHTML anywhere). QA P1s verified closed in source: ToggleSwitch rollback announces correct restored state, timing equalizer now uses jittered [500,700]ms for non-allowlisted and Promise.all floor of 600ms for allowlisted (no longer distinguishable), skip-links present on both /admin and /admin/login. 4 trivial fixes applied inline (<3 lines each, no auth-logic changes). 3 warnings remain as findings (all previously known QA P2s that need larger refactors or explicit design decisions).

security_audit_checklist.service_role_key_leak: PASS — SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY only referenced in src/lib/supabase/admin.ts. createAdminClient is only imported from sync.ts, src/app/admin/actions.ts, src/app/admin/page.tsx, and src/lib/sync/sync.ts — all server files. Runtime `typeof window` backstop also present.
security_audit_checklist.server_action_authorization: PASS — toggleFeatured, toggleHidden, refreshRepos, and requestMagicLink all start with assertAdmin() (for mutating actions) or isAllowlisted() (for magic-link). No mutation path bypasses the check.
security_audit_checklist.get_handlers_do_not_mutate: PASS — /auth/callback GET only exchanges code + signs out on denial; /admin/logout GET returns 405; no other GET performs DB writes.
security_audit_checklist.hardcoded_secrets: PASS — grep for api_key/secret/password/token found no literals. .env.example uses empty placeholders.
security_audit_checklist.sql_injection: PASS — all DB access goes through Supabase client's parameterized query builder (.eq, .update, .select). No raw SQL strings.
security_audit_checklist.xss_user_input: PASS — MagicLinkConfirmation renders submittedEmail inside <strong> (React auto-escapes). No dangerouslySetInnerHTML anywhere in src/. After inline fix, decoded email is also regex-validated so arbitrary strings can't leak into the confirmation screen as a phishing surface.
security_audit_checklist.open_redirect: PASS — /auth/callback always redirects to hard-coded /admin or /admin/login?error=denied; Server Action redirect targets are literal paths; no request.nextUrl.searchParams ever used as a redirect destination.
security_audit_checklist.safe_href_scheme_guard: PASS — safeHref accepts only http:/https:. Verified behavior for javascript:, data:, file:, vbscript: (new URL parses them but protocol filter rejects), for '//evil.com' (throws in new URL with no base → returns undefined), for null/undefined/empty (early return), for malformed URLs (try/catch returns undefined). Consumed by IndexRow AND AdminRepoRow — both fall back to '#' + aria-disabled + tabIndex=-1.
security_audit_checklist.max_w_container_dry: PASS — no remaining `max-w-[1100px]` literals in src/. All 7 consumers (Hero, SiteHeader, SiteFooter, ProjectIndex, AdminHeader, AdminPage, AdminLogin) use `max-w-container`. --container-container: 1100px registered in @theme so the utility is generated.
security_audit_checklist.admin_noindex: PASS — /admin and /admin/login both declare `robots: { index: false, follow: false }` via generateMetadata. proxy.ts also sets X-Robots-Tag: noindex, nofollow on every /admin* response.
security_audit_checklist.session_defense_in_depth: PASS — three layers: (1) proxy.ts redirects unauthenticated /admin* to /admin/login, (2) /admin/page.tsx re-verifies session + allowlist and signs out on mismatch, (3) every server action runs assertAdmin() as its first line. A middleware bypass still hits layers 2 and 3.
security_audit_checklist.qa_p1_toggle_rollback: PASS — ToggleSwitch.tsx:62 now correctly uses `setLiveMessage(prev ? srOn : srOff)` in catch block so screen readers announce the restored state, not a coincidentally-wrong fixed srOff.
security_audit_checklist.qa_p1_timing_equalizer: PASS — login/actions.ts uses randomEqualizerDelay() in [500,700] for the non-allowlisted branch and Promise.all([signInWithOtp, sleep(600)]) on the allowlisted branch. Both branches now overlap in the [600,900]ms region rather than the original fixed-300ms-vs-variable disparity.
security_audit_checklist.qa_p1_admin_skip_links:
PASS — /admin/page.tsx and /admin/login/page.tsx both render `Pular para conteúdo` above the AdminHeader, with id='admin-main' on
.

issues.issue: POST /admin/logout has no Origin/Referer check. Next.js's built-in Server Action CSRF protection does NOT cover Route Handlers — a cross-origin `<form action='https://mateushr.com/admin/logout' method='post'>` auto-submitted via JS on a malicious site would log the user out. Impact is low (logout is not destructive to data, session is recoverable) but violates defense-in-depth and QA's P2 finding is still open.
issues.fix: Option A (preferred): convert the logout handler to a Server Action so Next.js's origin check applies. Refactor AdminHeader to use `<form action={logoutAction}>` with the action defined in src/app/admin/actions.ts, and delete route.ts. Option B (minimal): before `supabase.auth.signOut()`, verify `request.headers.get('origin') === request.nextUrl.origin` (or fall back to host equality on the Referer URL) and return 403 on mismatch. Not applied inline because this touches auth-boundary logic and should be reviewed separately.

issues.issue: rateMap leaks empty-array keys over long-running lambdas. Abandoned keys whose entries all expire but that never receive another request stay in the Map forever. Per-email-only keying (no per-IP complement) also means a single attacker with N distinct emails never hits a single-email bucket. QA P2 still open. Not load-bearing for a single-admin allowlist, but a memory-and-bypass hygiene issue.
issues.fix: Add an opportunistic prune step at the end of underRateLimit() (walk 1-2 other keys per call and delete entries where `filter` returns []) OR migrate the rate-limit store to Vercel KV / Upstash. Not applied inline — the opportunistic prune is 4+ lines in the hot path and touches auth rate-limit logic, so it deserves a focused follow-up rather than a drive-by edit.

issues.issue: `console.error('[admin/toggleFeatured] update failed', error.message)` logs the raw Supabase error.message. Supabase errors sometimes include table/column metadata and RLS policy names — fine for a single-admin project but nonetheless a schema-leak surface if Vercel logs are ever shared with third parties.
issues.fix: Wrap the message: `console.error('[admin/toggleFeatured] update failed', { code: error.code, hint: '<redacted>' })` or log only `error.code`. Not applied inline — redaction policy is a project decision worth explicit discussion rather than a silent edit.

issues.issue: Phase auto-resets to idle 3s after success/error regardless of focus. If the keyboard user tabs to the button at second 2.9 they see 'atualizado · 12 repos' then the label shifts to 'atualizar agora' under them. aria-live already announced the state, so it's not a blocker — just a minor flicker.
issues.fix: Pause the reset timer when the button has :focus-within, or extend the timeout to 5s. Nice-to-have; current behavior is acceptable.

issues.issue: On mobile (< md) the two toggle sections render visible mono captions 'destacar' / 'ocultar', but the GitHub-arrow column only carries an aria-label — no visible 'github' caption. Asymmetric with the toggle labels. Not a WCAG failure (the arrow is decorative and the anchor is labelled), just a small pattern break.
issues.fix: Add `github` next to the arrow anchor to match the destacar/ocultar pattern.

fixes_applied_inline.what: Re-validate base64-decoded email against the same EMAIL_REGEX the Server Action uses. Empty string is returned (which triggers the form fallback) when the decoded payload isn't a well-formed email ≤254 chars. Closes QA P2 about phishing-page weaponization via crafted ?sent=1&email=<b64>.
Language fallback changed from 'TypeScript' to '—' so a non-TS repo with a missing language column isn't mislabeled. Matches QA P3 recommendation.
fixes_applied_inline.diff:
-  const language = repo.language ?? "TypeScript";
+  const language = repo.language ?? "—";

fixes_applied_inline.what: Defensive null-guard on pushed_at sort. The Database type marks pushed_at as NOT NULL, but guarding `(x ?? '').localeCompare(...)` costs nothing and immunizes the UI from a schema migration that ever nulls the column. Addresses QA P2 sort fragility.

gates.next_build: PASS — compiled in 3.9s, 6 routes generated, Proxy (Middleware) recognized
gates.review_script_tailwind: PASS — 0 findings from tailwind_canonical_linter (no bg-gradient-*, no arbitrary-value classes with canonical equivalents)
gates.secret_scan: PASS — no hardcoded credentials in src/

recommendation: APPROVE for PR. The 3 warnings are all previously-known QA P2s that carry over — two of them (logout CSRF, rate-map leak) should be addressed in a focused follow-up (tracked in open_questions). The third (PII log boundary) is a single-admin-project-acceptable issue that can be deferred until the app grows beyond one user. The 2 suggestions are pure nice-to-haves.
